content: The 2024-2025 SOTA push for scalable long-term memory in active learning agents can be reframed by treating the agent’s own memory system as a nested secondary active learning agent that autonomously curates which episodic traces to retain, compress, or retrieve based on intrinsic curiosity signals. This nested architecture means the primary task-driven agent only needs to annotate high-value memory retention choices rather than manually pre-defining memory management rules.
